canonical_definition: A statistical bias where a model learns a spurious correlation between the length of an input and its corresponding class label, using length as a shallow, discriminatory feature.
papers: PAT: Parameter-Free Audio-Text Aligner to Boost Zero-Shot Audio Classification
Padding Tone: A Mechanistic Analysis of Padding Tokens inT2IModels
